lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: What do you want?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I'm Vicks, squire to Sir Culler Flife. He's away on a surveillance mission to Burthorpe, so he sent me down here to do some cleaning.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Don't tell the knights, but I'm just not in the mood for it! I've served for years, and worked really hard, but they're not even thinking of making me a knight.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I don't know what the problem is. At my last review, I heard the examiner muttering something about my name, but I've no idea what he meant by that.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Keeping an eye on those Imperial Guard. We can't trust 'em, you know. But I shouldn't talk about his mission.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this place.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: This place? It's the castle crypt. The greatest White Knights are buried here, after a lifetime in the service of Saradomin. At least, that's how it used to be.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: When I was a new recruit, they brought me here and told me that if I served Saradomin faithfully, and with distinction, maybe I'd earn the honour of burial in this place too.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: But if you think about it, that's never going to happen - there just isn't space for more tombs! Anyway, my plan is to avoid dying, and maybe marry one of the barmaids when I retire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the glowing portal thing?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: It's because of something the Temple Knights have, called the Gaze of Saradomin. They get the option of being brought to Falador when they die, so Death has to maintain a presence in the area.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: That portal takes you to Death's own realm. He's got portals in all the places where people go when they die - Lumbridge, Edgeville and the rest. I hate it - the thing's terrifying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you alone.

sections.title: After Recruitment Drive when the player is part of the Temple Knights

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Can I help you, [sir/madam]?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I'm Vicks, squire to Sir Culler Flife. He's away on a surveillance mission to Burthorpe, so he sent me down here to do some cleaning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't seem to be cleaning much.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Don't tell the knights, but I'm just not in the mood for it! I've served for years, and worked really hard, but they're not even thinking of making me a knight.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: I don't know what the problem is. At my last review, I heard the examiner muttering something about my name, but I've no idea what he meant by that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your master doing in Burthorpe?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: Keeping an eye on those Imperial Guard, [sir/madam]. We can't trust 'em, you know. But I shouldn't talk about his mission, even with a knight such as yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this place.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: This place? It's the castle crypt. The greatest White Knights are buried here, after a lifetime in the service of Saradomin. At least, that's how it used to be.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: When I was a new recruit, they brought me here and told me that if I served Saradomin faithfully, and with distinction, maybe I'd earn the honour of burial in this place too.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: But if you think about it, that's never going to happen - there just isn't space for more tombs! Anyway, my plan is to avoid dying, and maybe marry one of the barmaids when I retire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the glowing portal thing?

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: It's because of the Gaze of Saradomin. You Temple Knights get the option of being brought to Falador when you die, so Death has to maintain a presence in the area.

lines.speaker: Squire
lines.text: That portal takes you to Death's own realm. He's got portals in all the places where people go when they die - Lumbridge, Edgeville and the rest. I hate it - the thing's terrifying!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you alone.
